content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Honorable George C. Hardie 
Mayor, City of Cathedral City 
68-625 Perez Road 
Cathedral City, CA 
92234 
October 27, 1988 
Re: 
Your Request For Advice 
Our File No. A-88-360 
Dear Mayor Hardie: 
You requested advice about application of conflict of 
interest provisions of .the Political Reform Act (the "Act").!! 
to your duties on the City Council of the City of Cathedral 
city. 
Over the telephone on October 24, 1988, you told me that 
facts in your letter have changed. 
The city council will not 
be considering placement on the ballot of a measure authorizing 
card clubs. 
Instead, you as a private citizen will be 
sponsoring a petition campaign to put the measure on the 
ballot. 
Consequently, part of your advice request now is 
moot. 
Nevertheless, you still need clarification about 
application of Government Code section 1090 to a city council 
decision about a card club license application. 
The 
Commission, however, does not administer this section. 
We 
refer you to your city attorney or to the Office of the 
Attorney General for advice regarding Section 1090 . 
.!! 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 .. Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


Honorable George C. Hardie 
October 27, 1988 
Page -2-
I am sorry it took so long to respond to your advice 
request. 
Please call me at (916) 322-5901 if you have any 
questions about this letter.

Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
GeI)eral Counsel

r 
•

'By: 
I Margar]. ta Al tam].rano 
Counsel, Legal Division 


September 12, 1988 
California Fair Political Practices commission 
428 J street, suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95804-0807 
Gentlemen: 
I am the mayor of Cathedral city, California. 
I was elected as a 
councilman in April, 1988, and elected by my peers to the office 
of mayor for a one year term. 
In January of 1987, as a businessman, I made a proposal to the 
city of Cathedral city regarding the establishment of a card club 
and hotel operation on property that I own in the city at 69-375 
Ramon Drive. 
This property currently houses a small hotel. 
My 
proposal was rejected by several members of the council who were 
opposed to gambling. 
At that time the issue was dropped. 
Recently, 
this issue has 
surfaced with what appears to be 
sUbstantial public support and, therefore, my questions are: 
i. 
Can I, as mayor and a potential license applicant, vote 
to place the issue before the voters? 
As you know, the 
question for a card club is asked simply, 'Shall there 
be card clubs in the City?' 
2. 
What input, if any, may I as mayor and as a potential 
license applicant, have in the drafting of an enabling 
ordinance? 
I believe it is the intention to place a 
full draft of the ordinance also before the voters. 
3. 
As a private citizen with my own funds, I assume that I 
am accorded the same rights as other citizens and may 
actively and financially support the passage of the 
ordinance. 


September 12, 1988 
Page 2 
4. 
I would also need a clarification on 1090 (?) of the 
Government Code. 
It is obviously self-evident that I 
can 
have 
no 
involvement 
in 
the license 
approval 
process, but would this license to operate a card club 
be considered a "contract" ? 
If it is deemed to be a contract, would I 
resign as mayor if I made application for the 
At what point would I 
resign 
when the 
application is made or when it was granted? 
have to 
license? 
license 
5. 
Is there such a thing as a "leave of absence"? If I am 
rejected as the licensee, 
could I 
then resume 
my 
mayoralty duties? 
6. 
If I 
am compelled to resign and am the successful 
applicant, at some point in the future, may I run for 
public office in this city again? 
7. 
If this is placed be:r:ore the voters by a petition drive 
that I sponsor, outside of the council directing it to 
the ballot, 
would 
this affect 
any 
of the 
above 
parameters? 
This issue is now here and time is of the essence. 
I know you 
get many requests for rulings, but I would appreciate a response 
on this as soon as possible. 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Honorable George G. Hardie 
Mayor, city of Cathedral city 
68-625 Perez Road 
Cathedral city, CA 
92234 
Dear Mayor Hardie: 
September 19, 1988 
Re: 
88-360 
Your letter requesting advice under the political Reform 
Act was received on September 14, 1988 by the Fair political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact Margarita Altamirano, an 
attorney in the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to information needed. 
If your request is for 
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 
18329) .) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
DMG:plh 
Very truly yours, 
I-
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
I 
: 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916) 322,5660
